title: Lathyrus albus
intro: Lathyrus albus, commonly known as the white pea or sweet pea, is a perennial legume native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and edible seeds. The seeds of Lathyrus albus have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lathyrus albus include alkaloids, such as lathyrine and beta-oxalyl-L-alpha-amino acid, as well as polyphenols and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Lathyrus albus is often prepared as a decoction or tea to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and is also used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Lathyrus albus is classified within the family Fabaceae. This family is commonly known as the legume or pea family. The genus Lathyrus is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Northern Hemisphere. Lathyrus albus is a species of white pea or grass pea, and it belongs to the subgenus Lathyrus. It is a perennial legume with a climbing habit.
uses: Lathyrus albus, also known as the white pea or sweet pea, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. properties: Lathyrus albus is known for its potential therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These compounds are responsible for the plant's potential medicinal properties and may contribute to its use in traditional medicine. parts: Lathyrus albus, also known as the white pea or white vetchling, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family. Lathyrus albus contains saponins, which are toxic compounds that can be used as a medicinal agent. However, they can also be toxic to humans in large doses, so proper caution must be taken when using this plant for medicinal purposes. The plant's seeds, leaves, and flowers can be used as medicinal parts, but more research is needed to fully understand their effects and potential uses. preparations: Lathyrus albus, also known as the white peavine or sweet pea, is used in herbal preparations, primarily for its potential health benefits. side_effects: Lathyrus albus contains a toxin called ODAP that can cause neurolathyrism, a condition characterized by muscle spasms, paralysis, and respiratory problems when consumed in large quantities over a prolonged period. Prolonged consumption of the plant can lead to damage to the nervous system. The toxin can also cause liver and kidney damage. In some cases, it may also cause birth defects if consumed during pregnancy. However, it is worth noting that the effects of consuming Lathyrus albus are generally seen in areas where it is a staple food and consumed in large quantities over time, rather than as an isolated incident.
